channel.title: NYT > World News
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Iran Live Updates: Israel Begins ‘Broad Wave of Strikes’ on Iran’s Infrastructure
item.description: The U.S. announced the names of four soldiers who were killed by an Iranian drone on Sunday. The leader of the U.S. Central Command said more than 50,000 troops, 200 fighters, two aircraft carriers and bombers were participating in the fighting.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times

item.title: Israel Seizes Chance to Crush Hezbollah and Remake Mideast Amid Iran War
item.description: With the war against Iran underway, and the U.S. military as a powerful ally, the Israeli government is seizing its chance to move against other adversaries.
item.dc:creator: David M. Halbfinger and Ronen Bergman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: A worker breaking down structural steel after American airstrikes on Tehran on Monday.

item.title: U.S. Hunts for Militants as the Nigerian Military Is Accused of Inaction
item.description: The country has one of the most feared armed forces in Africa, yet within its own borders, it has repeatedly failed to stop abductions and attacks.
item.dc:creator: Saikou Jammeh, Ruth Maclean, Dickson Adama and Ismail Auwal
item.media:description: Razed homes are seen days after an attack that left dozens dead in the Muslim-majority village of Woro, Nigeria, last month.

item.title: In Myanmar, Junta Leader Eyes the Title of President
item.description: The military ruler of Myanmar, Senior Gen. Min Aung Hlaing, is expected to seek a civilian post to add a veneer of legitimacy after sham elections.
item.dc:creator: Richard C. Paddock
item.category: Coups D'Etat and Attempted Coups D'Etat
item.media:description: Senior Gen. Min Aung Hlaing leaving a polling station in Naypyidaw, Myanmar, in December.

item.title: U.S. Attacks on Iran Test Fragile Truce With China
item.description: Beijing has condemned the U.S.-backed strikes on Iran, a close partner. Yet with trade talks looming, it is unlikely to risk a rupture with Washington.
item.dc:creator: David Pierson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: China’s leader, Xi Jinping, right, and the country’s top diplomat, Wang Yi, in Beijing last month.

item.title: Germany Has Ratcheted Up Defense Spending. In Europe, That Worries Some People.
item.description: Soon the country’s armed forces budget could exceed those of Britain and France combined. In Paris, there are concerns that European “strategic autonomy” will have a German accent.
item.dc:creator: Steven Erlanger
item.media:description: A training drill in Ahlen, Germany, in November. The country is already spending much more money on its military than its European partners are, according to spending trackers

item.title: When Trump Meets Germany’s Merz, Iran Will Likely Dominate Discussion
item.description: Chancellor Friedrich Merz’s visit to Washington, long planned, is now likely to be dominated by discussions of the attack, which Europeans did not take part in.
item.dc:creator: Jim Tankersley
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Chancellor Friedrich Merz of Germany, left, with President Trump in October.

item.title: Iran’s Strategy: Expand the War, Increase the Cost, Outlast Trump
item.description: The Islamic Republic is aiming to draw out the conflict and broaden the fighting. That would force President Trump to risk more casualties and more political capital.
item.dc:creator: Steven Erlanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The aftermath of a strike in Doha, Qatar, on Sunday. Iran is working to enlarge the battlefield.

item.title: As Trump Bombs Iran, America’s Allies Watch Fitfully From Sidelines
item.description: Disregarded by President Trump over Iran, Europe’s leaders are adapting to a world in which they are little more than bystanders.
item.dc:creator: Mark Landler
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Strikes on Tehran on Sunday.

item.title: Pakistan Strikes Bagram Air Base, Satellite Images Show
item.description: Satellite imagery showed flattened warehouses at the base that served as the nerve center of America’s 20-year war in Afghanistan, a prized asset for the Taliban.
item.dc:creator: Elian Peltier, Christiaan Triebert and Yaqoob Akbary

item.title: After Attacks in Iran, Cubans Worry They Might Be Trump’s Next Target
item.description: Many Cubans are wondering if the Trump administration plans to target their country’s Communist government next.
item.dc:creator: Ed Augustin, Frances Robles and David C. Adams
item.media:description: A line of cars waits outside a gas station in Havana last month. Cuba is suffering from a severe energy shortage largely brought on by a U.S. blockade of foreign oil shipments,

item.title: How the Bombing of Iran Is Affecting Lebanon, Kuwait and Other Countries
item.description: Fighting is spreading throughout the Middle East as Iran retaliates against a huge American-Israeli military campaign.
item.dc:creator: Amelia Nierenberg
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Munir Uz Zaman/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: Stranded passengers outside the main international airport in Dhaka, Bangladesh’s capital. The conflict has severely disrupted air travel.

item.title: Hezbollah Plunges Lebanon Back Into War Despite Its Weakness
item.description: The militant group’s attacks, apparently at the behest of Iran, led to retaliation from Israel and were “practically a suicide mission” for Hezbollah, an analyst said.
item.dc:creator: Christina Goldbaum
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Hezbollah supporters rallied in Beirut, Lebanon, on Sunday in solidarity with Iran after U.S. and Israeli strikes killed Iran’s supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

item.title: Carney Hails New Ties Between Canada and India, Setting Aside Rift Over Killing
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney focused on business during a meeting with his Indian counterpart, Narendra Modi, with little mention of India’s alleged role in a deadly shooting in 2023.
item.dc:creator: Ian Austen
item.media:description: Prime Minister Mark Carney of Canada, left, with the Indian leader, Narendra Modi, in New Delhi on Monday.

item.title: Here is the latest.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi and Yan Zhuang

item.title: What to Know About Nepal's Gen Z Election
item.description: Nepal is holding its first election since a youth-led uprising against corruption toppled the government last year. Here is what to know.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Beech and Binod Ghimire
item.media:credit: Hannah Reyes Morales for The New York Times
item.media:description: People attending a campaign rally in Kathmandu, Nepal, on Saturday.

item.title: Where are China’s A.I. Doomers?
item.description: Chinese policymakers and the public have expressed high levels of optimism about A.I., even as many in the West worry about the technology’s effects on employment or humanity in general.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Wang
item.media:credit: Qilai Shen for The New York Times
item.media:description: Service robots on display at the World Artificial Intelligence Conference in Shanghai last year.

item.title: Toronto Synagogue Is Damaged by Gunfire on Night of Jewish Holiday
item.description: The temple said 20 shots were fired at the building but no one was hurt.
item.dc:creator: Rylee Kirk

item.title: Iran Strikes U.S. Military Communication Infrastructure in Mideast
item.description: Satellite images show damage near vital equipment on sites in at least five countries.
item.dc:creator: Devon Lum, Haley Willis and Riley Mellen
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

item.title: Carney Says That Canada’s Support for Airstrikes Came ‘With Regret’
item.description: The prime minister criticized Israel and the U.S. for not consulting other nations or the U.N. before their attack on Iran.
item.dc:creator: Ian Austen
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Canada’s prime minister, Mark Carney, said Iran had persisted in its nuclear ambitions in defiance of years of diplomatic efforts, but he also criticized the United States and Israel.

item.title: Who Is Mojtaba Khamenei, the Son and Possible Successor of Iran’s Supreme Leader?
item.description: The selection of the son of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, who was killed in a strike on his compound on Saturday, could anger Iranians seeking change.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.media:credit: Office of The Iranian Supreme Leader/Wana News Agency, via Reuters
item.media:description: A handout image from Iran state media of Mojtaba Khamenei, a son of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, in Tehran in 2024.

item.title: These American Service Members Died in the Iran Conflict
item.description: They were from Nebraska, Florida, Iowa and Minnesota. One was wrapping up his final deployment and hoped to open a martial arts studio.
item.dc:creator: Corina Knoll, Christina Morales, Pooja Salhotra and Susan C. Beachy
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Trump Tries to Quiet Claims Among Supporters That Israel Dragged Him Into War
item.description: Many of President Trump’s allies have urged him and his Make America Great Again movement to shift away from their close ties to Israel and military entanglements in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Tyler Pager
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: President Trump attempted to extinguish the idea that Israel forced the United States to enter a war with Iran.

item.title: Americans were urged to leave 14 countries in the region.
item.dc:creator: Lazaro Gamio

item.title: Maps show wide-ranging attacks in the opening days of the conflict.
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

item.title: Who’s in Charge of Iran’s Government After Khamenei’s Killing?
item.description: Who is in charge of Iran’s government after airstrikes killed the supreme leader and some of his defense and security officials? Erika Solomon, our bureau chief for Iran and Iraq, describes what we know.
item.dc:creator: Erika Solomon, Nikolay Nikolov, Jon Miller, Stephanie Swart, Rafaela Balster, Samuel Granados and Christina Shaman

item.title: How Trump Decided to Go to War
item.description: President Trump’s embrace of military action in Iran was set off by the determination of Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of Israel to end diplomatic negotiations. Our investigative reporter Mark Mazzetti describes how few of Mr. Trump’s advisers voiced opposition.
item.dc:creator: Mark Mazzetti, Leila Medina, Sutton Raphael, Joey Sendaydiego, Jon Hazell and Rafaela Balster
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Iranians Viewed by U.S. as Potential Leaders Died in Strikes, Trump Says
item.description: With the future of Iran deeply uncertain, President Trump acknowledged that it could end up with a ruler “as bad” as Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.
item.dc:creator: Jim Tankersley, Aaron Boxerman, Christina Goldbaum and Michael Levenson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Tehran after airstrikes on Tuesday. President Trump said that he had made the decision to go to war to pre-empt Iran, which he claimed was about to attack.

item.title: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei’s Son Emerges as Leading Choice to Be His Successor
item.description: If Mojtaba Khamenei is chosen by Iran’s senior clerics, it may signal a victory for hard-liners, an analyst said.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Mojtaba Khamenei, son of the slain former leader of Iran, in Tehran in 2019.

item.title: Nearly 900 People Have Been Killed in Fighting in the Middle East
item.description: The vast majority of deaths reported so far have been in Iran, but the human toll is being felt around the world.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Smoke rising on Tuesday after a strike west of Tehran.

item.title: Trump Letter to Congress Justifying Iran Strikes Outlines No Imminent Threat
item.description: The president told lawmakers that the objective of the assault was to “neutralize Iran’s malign activities,” but did not present evidence of immediate plans by Tehran to attack the United States.
item.dc:creator: Megan Mineiro
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: A letter from President Trump submitted to Congress on Monday said the military attack on Iran was designed to “neutralize Iran’s malign activities.”

item.title: Rubio Walks Back Suggestion That Israel Forced U.S. Hand in Iran Strikes
item.description: A day earlier, the secretary of state had said that the decision to attack Iran was based on knowledge that Israel was going to strike, and an assumption that Iran would target U.S. forces as a result.
item.dc:creator: Robert Jimison
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio speaking to reporters on Capitol Hill on Tuesday.

item.title: Trump Says U.S. Navy Might Escort Oil and Gas Tankers in Persian Gulf
item.description: Shipping companies are not sending oil and gas through the Strait of Hormuz because they fear their ships might be attacked.
item.dc:creator: Peter Eavis
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: President Trump said the U.S. Navy was prepared to escort tankers through the Strait of Hormuz. The price of oil fell slightly after his announcement.

item.title: A Fight About the Future of War
item.description: A.I. is already reshaping warfare, but there are big disagreements over what guardrails are needed.
item.dc:creator: Katrin Bennhold
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Marissa Leshnov for The New York Times

item.title: British Columbia Moving to Permanent Daylight Saving Time, Changing Clocks for the Last Time Sunday
item.description: The Canadian province is moving permanently to daylight saving time, officials announced.
item.dc:creator: Aimee Ortiz
item.media:description: The Gastown Steam Clock marking the noon hour in Vancouver, British Columbia.

item.title: Trump Lays Out His ‘Worst Case’ Scenario in Iran
item.description: Who will lead Iran once the war is over? “Most of the people we had in mind are dead,” says President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Shawn McCreesh
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: A black flag flying over Tehran on Monday after Ayatollah Khamenei was killed in U.S.-Israeli strikes.

item.title: Trump Threatens to End Trade With Spain
item.description: His anger stems from the country’s denial of the use of its military bases to U.S. forces involved in the attack against Iran. Spain didn’t seem fazed.
item.dc:creator: Jason Horowitz
item.media:description: President Trump had several complaints about Spain, including that it has not increased its NATO defense spending to a level he set.

item.title: How Iran Is Using Drones to Retaliate Against the U.S. and Allies
item.description: The Pentagon and Middle Eastern countries say that most of the drones have been intercepted. But some have slipped through and caused damage.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt, Helene Cooper and Sheera Frenkel
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Smoke rising from a building that was hit in an Iranian drone strike in Manama, Bahrain, on Saturday.

item.title: Thousands in Iran Attend Burial of Children Killed in Bombing of School
item.description: There were students attending classes at the time that the school was destroyed. Some 175 people were killed by the attack on the girls’ elementary school.
item.dc:creator: Malachy Browne and Pranav Baskar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Abbas Zakeri/Mehr News Agency, via Associated Press
item.media:description: A picture made available by Iranian state-run media of the funeral of the victims of a strike on an elementary school. The school, adjacent to a naval base, was in session on Saturday when an airstrike hit it, killing 175 people, Iranian officials and rights groups said.

item.title: Trapped Between U.S. and Iran, Gulf Countries Face Nightmare Scenario
item.description: The energy-rich nations that sought to avert the American-Israeli war on Iran have been sucked into the spiraling conflict.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Nereim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: An explosion in an industrial zone in the United Arab Emirates on Tuesday. Iran has fired more than a thousand missiles and drones at Saudi Arabia, the Emirates, Qatar, Bahrain, Kuwait and Oman.

item.title: Emirati Leaders, Seeking to Project Calm, Go to the Mall
item.description: A video released by Dubai’s government of the Emirati president visiting a mall underscored the authoritarian country’s efforts to maintain its placid reputation amid spiraling violence.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Wolfe
item.media:description: The Dubai Mall on Tuesday. Emirati politicians filmed a video there on Monday.

item.title: Israeli Plans Include Option of Going Deeper Into Lebanon, Officials Say
item.description: Israel said it deployed more forces in Lebanon to protect its people, but its military chief said the goal is more ambitious: to disarm Hezbollah. Israel made plans for the incursion well in advance.
item.dc:creator: Natan Odenheimer and David M. Halbfinger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The site of an Israeli strike in Sidon, Lebanon, on Tuesday.

item.title: Here’s where Israel warned people to evacuate in southern Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Elena Shao

item.title: Western Governments Rush to Help Stranded Travelers in the Middle East
item.description: Hundreds of thousands have been stranded since the conflict started. The United States urged Americans to leave and said on Tuesday it was “actively working on plans” to help them do so.
item.dc:creator: Isabella Kwai, Ceylan Yeğinsu, Omnia Al Desoukie and Alexandra E. Petri
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Airplanes parked at Dubai International Airport on Monday. Travelers from Europe and the United States were rushing to find routes out of the Middle East, as the conflict continued.

item.title: A Critical Nuclear Site in Iran Is Spared During U.S.-Israeli Airstrikes, for Now
item.description: The Isfahan facility, suspected of storing a cache of enriched uranium, was smashed during the 12-day campaign last June.
item.dc:creator: William J. Broad
item.media:description: A uranium processing plant in Isfahan, which hosts one of Iran’s major nuclear sites, seen in 2005 during its early stages of development. Experts suspect that mountains near the site, like those to the right, now harbor a complex of tunnels where Iran keeps a batch of uranium sufficient to make nine or 10 atomic bombs.

item.title: U.S. and Israel Striking Iran Security Agencies That Helped Crush Protests
item.description: Their attacks have targeted police stations and intelligence bases in what may be part of a plan to encourage Iranians to rise up against the government, experts say.
item.dc:creator: Erika Solomon and Sanjana Varghese
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: A police station destroyed by U.S.-Israel airstrikes, seen during a government media tour on Tuesday.

item.title: Stocks Drop as Inflation Risk Emerges in Wake of Iran War
item.description: The S&P 500 recovered ground in the afternoon but still ended the day lower, as investors sought cover from the unpredictable effects that the fighting could have on the world economy.
item.dc:creator: Joe Rennison and Jason Karaian
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: As Iran Retaliates, U.S. Closes Embassies in Saudi Arabia and Kuwait
item.description: The State Department also ordered nonessential staff in the United Arab Emirates, Qatar, Bahrain, Jordan and Iraq to to leave amid further retaliation from Iran.
item.dc:creator: Yan Zhuang
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Kuwait on Monday.

item.title: Israel Advances in Lebanon and Seizes More Land, as Hezbollah Fight Escalates
item.description: The Israeli military said troops advanced to protect border towns from Hezbollah attacks, spurring fears that the government is weighing a wider ground war.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman and Christina Goldbaum
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Israeli soldiers near the border with Lebanon on Monday.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Jim Tankersley, Shawn McCreesh, Anton Troianovski and Joe Rennison

item.title: In a World Order Defined by Trump, the Key to Europe’s Defense Is Germany
item.description: In the wake of the U.S. bombing of Iran and its dismissal of European allies, an anxious continent’s best chance at security runs through its largest economy.
item.dc:creator: Elisabeth Zerofsky

item.title: Global Economy Is Facing the Prospect of Another Profound Shock
item.description: A protracted conflict in the Middle East risks a spike in energy prices and broader inflation.
item.dc:creator: Peter S. Goodman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Firefighters at the Ras Tanura oil refinery in Saudi Arabia on Monday, after a drone attack. The Middle East is the source of 30 percent of the world’s oil.

item.title: ‘Pray We Make It Through the Night’: Iran’s Capital Under Siege
item.description: Many residents of Tehran managed to get out of town when the U.S. and Israel attack began, but others who could not described living under bombardment.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi and Arash Khamooshi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Destruction of Cultural Antiquities and Heritage
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: People are standing behind a yellow line after a police station on Niloufar street was hit by US-Israel attacks and damaged the next door buildings in Tehran on Monday.

item.title: Under Pressure from Trump, Cuban Leader Calls for ‘Urgent’ Economic Change
item.description: President Miguel Díaz-Canel said the private sector needed more autonomy, as the island confronts a U.S. oil blockade that has deepened a humanitarian crisis.
item.dc:creator: Luis Ferré-Sadurní and David C. Adams
item.media:description: President Miguel Díaz-Canel of Cuba, center, at a funeral in January for those killed in U.S. strikes on Venezuela.

item.title: U.S. Embassy in Saudi Capital Hit by 2 Drones on Tuesday, Kingdom Says
item.description: Americans in Riyadh and other cities were advised to shelter in place immediately.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Trump Embraces U.S. Military Power After Years of Caution
item.description: The wars in Afghanistan and Iraq offered a stark lesson in the limits of military force. The Iran attacks suggest an era of postwar wariness is over.
item.dc:creator: Greg Jaffe
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump in Corpus Christi, Texas, last week. His sudden embrace of U.S. military power is a stunning reversal.

item.title: Why Eliminating Iran’s Ballistic Missiles Could Be Difficult
item.description: Much of Tehran’s arsenal of missiles may be hidden underground, making it harder to destroy.
item.dc:creator: John Ismay
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
Arms Control and Limitation and Disarmament

item.title: Can Trump Legally Kill Iran’s Leader?
item.description: The United States and Israel started a war with Iran by killing its supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.
item.dc:creator: Charlie Savage
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: A woman holding a photo of Ayatollah Khamenei during a rally in Tehran last month.

item.title: U.S. Slaps Sanctions on Rwanda, Saying It Sabotaged Trump Peace Deal
item.description: Rwanda’s government responded by claiming the sanctions unjustly targeted only one party to the conflict and misrepresented the facts.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Mpoke Bigg
item.category: War Crimes, Genocide and Crimes Against Humanity
Human Rights and Human Rights Violations
item.media:description: President Trump, President Paul Kagame of Rwanda, center, and President Felix Tshisekedi of the Democratic Republic of Congo participate in a signing ceremony at the Institute of Peace in Washington in December 2025.

item.title: Trump Signals U.S. Is Prepared for a Long War Against Iran
item.description: President Trump also declined to rule out sending ground troops into Iran as the conflict spread into the Gulf countries and Lebanon and Israel exchanged strikes.
item.dc:creator: Shawn McCreesh, Tyler Pager, Eric Schmitt, Helene Cooper and Abdi Latif Dahir
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: People gathering their belongings at their damaged homes on Monday after U.S.-Israeli airstrikes hit a police station in Tehran. President Trump said that still-bigger waves of airstrikes were coming.

item.title: Rubio Warns Iran Faces ‘Hardest Hits’ Ahead, Offering Conflicting Rationales
item.description: The secretary of state signaled that an escalation in Iran was likely and said the attacks underway were both pre-emptive and defensive, describing a far more extensive mission than was initially laid out.
item.dc:creator: Robert Jimison and Megan Mineiro
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio told reporters on Capitol Hill on Monday that the “next phase will be even more punishing on Iran than it is right now.”
